result: PR #133 geomeTRIC optimizer backend is merged upstream. Treat as Done; any follow-up geomeTRIC issues should be new workstream cards.

title: DFTB+: real validation benchmark
body: Run 10–20 small molecules with a real dftb+ executable and Slater-Koster parameters. Validate OpenQP DFTB+ energy, gradient, and optimize paths through DFTBPlusRunner/OpenQP wrapper; upsert successful results to calc_db.json and prepare a compact benchmark report. Recommended first priority.

title: DFTB excited-state roadmap: TD-DFTB / SF-DFTB / spin-polarized DFTB
body: Define a concrete OpenQP+DFTB excited-state roadmap distinct from the current ground-state DFTB+ backend. Compare TD-DFTB, spin-flip DFTB, spin-polarized/unrestricted DFTB, and SCC-DFTB ingredients; identify which are supported by external dftb+ vs require new theory/runtime work.

title: TD-DFTB feasibility spike with external dftb+
body: Check current dftb+ support for TD-DFTB excitation energies, oscillator strengths, excited-state gradients, and output formats. Build a minimal H2O/ethylene smoke outside OpenQP first, then specify what parser/API contract OpenQP would need.

title: DFTB+: compare against xTB geometry workflow
body: Benchmark DFTB+ vs xTB vs OpenQP DFT optimized geometries. Metrics: bond/geometric RMSD, OpenQP single-point gradient norms, energy ordering, elapsed time, and robustness. Decide whether DFTB+ should replace or complement xTB for cheap geometry acquisition.

title: SF-DFTB theory and implementation reconnaissance
body: Investigate spin-flip DFTB variants in the literature and available implementations. Determine whether SF-DFTB can be implemented via DFTB+ outputs or needs a native OpenQP Hamiltonian/response module. Summarize equations, required intermediates, and validation systems.

title: MRSF-DFTB / mixed-reference spin-flip DFTB concept design
body: Explore whether OpenQP MRSF-TDDFT machinery can be adapted to a DFTB Hamiltonian. Identify minimal data structures: orbital energies/coefs, two-electron or gamma-matrix approximations, spin-coupling terms, Davidson sigma-vector changes, and expected limitations.

title: DFTB+: fast pre-optimizer for OpenQP/MRSF workflows
body: Use DFTB+ ground-state optimize as a fast S0 geometry source before OpenQP BHHLYP/6-31g* MRSF single-points or optimizations. Candidate systems: thymine, uracil, cyclobutanone, and rPSB-like molecules. Validate geometry before expensive OpenQP runs.

title: DFTB excited-state gradients and NAC capability audit
body: Audit TD-DFTB/DFTB+ and literature support for excited-state gradients, nonadiabatic couplings, derivative couplings, and surface hopping. Separate available external DFTB+ capabilities from features OpenQP would need to implement.

title: Spin-polarized/unrestricted DFTB ground-state backend extension
body: Before SF/TD extensions, assess adding spin-polarized or unrestricted DFTB support: multiplicity handling, spin constants, SK parameter requirements, output parsing, and validation on radicals/triplets.

title: DFTB+: PES scan pre-screening
body: Use DFTB+ for cheap scans along key coordinates before expensive OpenQP DFT/MRSF scans. Candidate coordinates: cyclobutanone ring opening/UED coordinate, thymine FC→CI path guesses, uracil puckering/bond stretch scans. Select promising points for higher-level OpenQP calculations.

title: DFTB parameter requirements for TD/SF work
body: Inventory Slater-Koster and parameter-set requirements for TD-DFTB, spin-polarized DFTB, organic photochemistry, radicals, and triplets. Compare 3ob/mio/matsci/other sets and note missing spin constants or element coverage.

title: OpenQP native DFTB Hamiltonian design spike
body: Sketch a native OpenQP DFTB/SCC-DFTB Hamiltonian path instead of only calling external dftb+. Include data model, parameter loading, gamma matrices, charges/SCC loop, gradients, and how TD/SF response would connect.

title: DFTB+: curated OpenQP examples and reference JSON
body: Build a curated examples/DFTB set with runnable inputs and matching reference JSON for install/test coverage. Include supported energy, grad, and optimize examples plus capability/limitation notes. This strengthens the DFTB+ feature PR.

title: TD-DFTB/SF-DFTB validation molecule set
body: Define validation systems and reference data for excited-state DFTB work: ethylene, butadiene, formaldehyde, uracil/thymine fragments, radicals/triplets, and conical-intersection or spin-flip test cases. Include target observables and references.

title: DFTB excited-state benchmark/report plan
body: Plan a benchmark comparing TD-DFTB/SF-DFTB candidates against OpenQP TDDFT/MRSF and available literature: excitation energies, state ordering, spin character/<S^2>, gradients, and runtime.

title: DFTB+: UED trajectory and diffraction pre-screening
body: Use DFTB+ to generate cheap ground-state or constrained structures for UED diffraction tests, especially cyclobutanone. Treat DFTB+ as structural pre-screening only unless/until TD-DFTB/excited-state dynamics support is validated.

title: DFTB+: real validation benchmark
body: Run 10–20 small molecules with a real dftb+ executable and Slater-Koster parameters. Validate OpenQP DFTB+ energy, gradient, and optimize paths through DFTBPlusRunner/OpenQP wrapper; upsert successful results to calc_db.json and prepare a compact benchmark report.

title: DFTB+: compare against xTB geometry workflow
body: Benchmark DFTB+ vs xTB vs OpenQP DFT optimized geometries. Metrics: bond/geometric RMSD, OpenQP single-point gradient norms, energy ordering, elapsed time, and robustness. Decide whether DFTB+ should replace or complement xTB for cheap geometry acquisition.

title: DFTB+: TD-DFTB/excited-state feasibility spike
body: Investigate whether external dftb+ can provide excited-state energies/gradients in a form OpenQP can consume. Keep this separate from the current ground-state DFTB+ branch. Higher risk; do after ground-state energy/grad/optimize validation is stable.

title: DFTB+: fast pre-optimizer for OpenQP/MRSF workflows
body: Use DFTB+ ground-state optimize as a fast S0 geometry source before OpenQP BHHLYP/6-31g* MRSF single-points or optimizations. Candidate systems: thymine, uracil, cyclobutanone, and rPSB-like molecules. Validate geometry before expensive OpenQP runs.

title: DFTB+: PES scan pre-screening
body: Use DFTB+ for cheap scans along key coordinates before expensive OpenQP DFT/MRSF scans. Candidate coordinates: cyclobutanone ring opening/UED coordinate, thymine FC→CI path guesses, uracil puckering/bond stretch scans. Select promising points for higher-level OpenQP calculations.

title: DFTB+: Slater-Koster parameter-set comparison
body: Compare relevant parameter sets such as 3ob, mio, matsci, and pbc for molecules of interest. Record parameter coverage, geometry quality, failures, and recommended defaults for organic photochemistry screening.

title: DFTB+: curated OpenQP examples and reference JSON
body: Build a curated examples/DFTB set with runnable inputs and matching reference JSON for install/test coverage. Include supported energy, grad, and optimize examples plus capability/limitation notes. This strengthens the DFTB+ feature PR.

title: DFTB+: UED trajectory and diffraction pre-screening
body: Use DFTB+ to generate cheap ground-state or constrained structures for UED diffraction tests, especially cyclobutanone. Treat DFTB+ as structural pre-screening only unless/until TD-DFTB/excited-state dynamics support is validated.

title: DFTB+: documentation/tutorial follow-up PR
body: After validation, write user-facing docs for README/PyOQP/wiki: setup, input format, supported runtype matrix, parameter requirements, examples, and known limitations. Keep docs split from unrelated feature work unless explicitly requested.

title: DFTB+: TD-DFTB/excited-state feasibility spike
body: Investigate whether external dftb+ can provide excited-state energies/gradients in a form OpenQP can consume. Keep this separate from the current ground-state DFTB+ branch. Higher risk; do after ground-state energy/grad/optimize validation is stable.

title: DFTB+: Slater-Koster parameter-set comparison
body: Compare relevant parameter sets such as 3ob, mio, matsci, and pbc for molecules of interest. Record parameter coverage, geometry quality, failures, and recommended defaults for organic photochemistry screening.

title: DFTB+: batch runner and calc_db ingestion workflow
body: Create a small workflow script or documented command pattern: XYZ/input → DFTB+ energy/grad/optimize → parse outputs → upsert calc_db.json. Use it to make DFTB+ routine as a screening backend.

title: DFTB+: documentation/tutorial follow-up PR
body: After validation, write user-facing docs for README/PyOQP/wiki: setup, input format, supported runtype matrix, parameter requirements, examples, and known limitations. Keep docs split from unrelated feature work unless explicitly requested.

It was not doing geomeTRIC optimizer or TD-DFTB coding. Next action should be explicitly re-scoped before any further coding run.

title: cron: openqp-dftbplus-backend-autopilot-v2
body: DFTB project tracker for the active openqp-dftbplus-backend-autopilot-v2 cron job. Scheduled means waiting for next cron tick; it moves to In progress while the cron run is active and Done only when the finite autopilot is actually complete.
